data.title: SumiSaujana Eyes MYR96 Million Via IPO
data.description:
SumiSaujana (KLSE:SUMI) is set to raise 96 million ringgit via its initial public offering on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia.

The IPO of the chemicals firm involves a public issue of 310 million shares and an offer for sale of 90 million shares at 0.24 ringgit apiece, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

Proceeds will be used for acquisitions, expansion, and capital expenditure, it added.

data.title: JTF International to Widen Loss in 2024; Shares Down 4%
data.description:
JTF International expects an attributable loss of between 8 million yuan and 10 million yuan for 2024, wider than 1.5 million yuan a year prior, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The oil and petrochemical product wholesaler attributed the anticipated wider loss mainly to a swing from gross profit to gross loss and a decrease in gross margin for through-port trade.

Shares of the company were down nearly 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Hebei Yichen Industrial to Swing to 2024 Loss
data.description:
Hebei Yichen Industrial Group expects a net loss of 50.8 million yuan for the year 2024, compared with a net gain of 50.3 million yuan a year prior, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The railway sleeper products provider attributed the anticipated swing to loss mainly to an impairment loss subject to provision, loss from changes in the fair value of financial assets held for trading, investment loss from the disposal of financial assets held for trading, and a lower gross profit of its products.

data.title: Strong Cresemba® (isavuconazole) sales performance in Asia Pacific and China triggers milestone payment to Basilea
data.description:
Allschwil, Switzerland, March 14, 2025

Basilea Pharmaceutica Ltd, Allschwil , a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company committed to meeting the needs of patients with severe bacterial and fungal infections, announced today that the continued strong sales performance of the antifungal Cresemba® (isavuconazole) by its license partner Pfizer Inc. in the Asia Pacific region and China exceeded the sales threshold triggering a USD 2.5 million milestone payment.

David Veitch, Basilea’s Chief Executive Officer, stated: “We are pleased with the strong sales performance from our partner Pfizer, resulting in this first sales milestone for the Asia Pacific and China region this year, which significantly surpasses previous sales milestones for the region. This underscores the continued progress in making Cresemba available to increasing numbers of patients suffering from life-threatening invasive mold infections in this region.”

The license agreement between Basilea and Pfizer for Cresemba covers Europe (excluding the Nordic countries) as well as China and 16 countries in the Asia Pacific region.

Cresemba is marketed in more than 70 countries. According to the latest available market data, total global in-market sales of Cresemba in the twelve-month period between October 2023 and September 2024 amounted to USD 533 million, a 20 percent growth year-on-year, making it the largest branded antifungal for invasive fungal infections worldwide.1

About Cresemba® (isavuconazole)

Cresemba, with the active ingredient isavuconazole, is an intravenous (i.v.) and oral azole antifungal. Basilea has entered into several license and distribution agreements for Cresemba covering approximately 115 countries. In China, the oral and intravenous formulations are approved for the treatment of adult patients with invasive aspergillosis and invasive mucormycosis. Isavuconazole is also approved in the European Union2, the United Kingdom3, the United States (US)4 and several additional countries including in the Asia Pacific region.5

About Basilea

Basilea is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company founded in 2000 and headquartered in Switzerland. We are committed to discovering, developing and commercializing innovative drugs to meet the needs of patients with severe bacterial or fungal infections. We have successfully launched two hospital brands, Cresemba for the treatment of invasive fungal infections and Zevtera for the treatment of bacterial infections. In addition, we have preclinical and clinical anti-infective assets in our portfolio. Basilea is listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange . Please visit basilea.com.

Disclaimer

This communication expressly or implicitly contains certain forward-looking statements, such as "believe", "assume", "expect", "forecast", "project", "may", "could", "might", "will" or similar expressions concerning Basilea Pharmaceutica Ltd, Allschwil and its business, including with respect to the progress, timing and completion of research, development and clinical studies for product candidates. Such statements involve certain known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors, which could cause the actual results, financial condition, performance or achievements of Basilea Pharmaceutica Ltd, Allschwil to be materially different from any future results, performance or achievements expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. Basilea Pharmaceutica Ltd, Allschwil is providing this communication as of this date and does not undertake to update any forward-looking statements contained herein as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

data.title: Strong Cresemba(R) (isavuconazole) sales performance in Asia Pacific and China triggers milestone payment to Basilea
Japan PM apologises for giving out gifts, clouding budget prospects
data.description:
Key points:
  • Ishiba says used pocket money to hand out gifts to LDP lawmakers
  • Move draws criticism, opposition party calls for PM to step down
  • Political uncertainty adds to challenges for enacting budget

Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba apologised on Friday for giving gift certificates to some ruling party lawmakers, a move that could hurt his administration's already low approval ratings and risk delaying passage of next fiscal year's budget.

The political uncertainty could cast doubt on Ishiba's leadership ahead of an upper house election set for July, and comes at a time when Japan's economy faces headwinds from the escalating trade war waged by U.S. President Donald Trump.

"Market volatility is heightening on uncertainty over U.S. and European economic policies. But now, market players may need to look more carefully at domestic political developments," said Yusuke Matsumoto, senior market economist at Mizuho Securities.

Speaking in parliament, Ishiba said he used "pocket money" to hand out gift certificates to 15 lawmakers of the Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) before having dinner with them on March 3 as a "show of appreciation" for their hard work getting elected.

Domestic media reported on Thursday that Ishiba handed gift certificates worth 100,000 yen ($673) each to the lawmakers. When asked by reporters later on Thursday whether he may step down, Ishiba said only that the gifts did not violate any law, according to the Nikkei newspaper.

"My action caused distrust and anger among many people, for which I deeply apologise," Ishiba told parliament on Friday in response to a question by a ruling party lawmaker.

While Ishiba said the move was not illegal as it was a personal gift with no political intentions, it drew criticism even from within the LDP's coalition partner and calls from some opposition parties for him to resign.

The gift issue adds to challenges for Ishiba's minority coalition, which has been forced to make rare amendments to the government's budget plan for the fiscal year beginning in April, to appease opposition parties and ensure its passage through parliament by the March 31 deadline.

Failure to pass the annual budget in time could force the government to compile a stop-gap budget, which will deal a blow to Ishiba's political standing, and hurt the economy by delaying spending plans, some analysts say.

Tetsushi Sakamoto, chairperson of the LDP's diet affairs committee, said on Friday the gift issue could dampen prospects for passing the budget by end-March, Kyodo news agency reported.

Japan will hold an election around July for the upper house, where the ruling coalition's slim majority could also be at risk if Ishiba cannot revive public trust roiled by a previous political issues over unrecorded donations to lawmakers.

A poll by public broadcaster NHK last week showed the Ishiba administration's approval rating stood at 36%, down from 44% in February.

Japan's economy expanded an annualised 2.8% in the final quarter of last year on robust business expenditure and consumption. But analysts polled by Reuters expect growth to slow to a meagre 0.4% as rising living costs and slowing global demand weigh on consumption and exports.

($1 = 148.5900 yen)

data.title: Jingyeda Technology's Unit Gets License to Train Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Operators
data.description:
Beijing Jingyeda Technology's subsidiary, Huailai Qidian Vocational Skill Training School, was licensed as a training institution for unmanned aerial vehicle or UAV operators, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The Civil Aviation Administration of China handed down the certification, the Chinese industry information systems provider said.

The school also licensed as a civil UAV pilot trainer, to operate UAVs and to operate UAV flight activities.

data.title: Swiss Life reports higher fee result
data.description:
Insurer and asset manager Swiss Life reported a higher annual fee result on Friday, citing the very strong performance of Swiss Life Asset Managers.

Switzerland's largest life insurance provider reported a net fee result of 875 million Swiss francs ($990.49 million) for the year, compared with a result of 658 million in 2023.

($1=0.8834 Swiss francs)

data.title: China's Luzhou Laojiao jumps as key shareholder plans to raise stake
data.description:
** Shares of Chinese liquor products manufacturer and distributor Luzhou Laojiao Co Ltd jump 7.2% to 145 yuan, their highest since December 12, and on course to snap two continuous sessions of fall

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct gain since November 7

** Shenzhen-listed Luzhou Laojiao says its controlling shareholder plans to raise its holding in the company for 150 mln-300 mln yuan ($20.71 mln - $41.42 mln) in next six months

** The blue-chip CSI 300 Index surges 2.3% and the Shanghai Composite Index rises 1.7%

** Stock up 15.5% YTD

($1 = 7.2430 Chinese yuan)

data.title: China Plans Press Conference on Consumption Boost
data.description:
Chinese officials will hold a press conference on Monday to discuss the country's measures to boost consumption, the State Council Information Office said Friday.

Officials from the finance, commerce, and human resources ministries, the People's Bank of China, and the country's market regulator, are expected to attend.

The press conference "fanned expectations on policy support," Bloomberg News reported separately, citing Shen Meng, a director at Beijing-based investment bank Chanson & Co.

However, if Monday's press briefing will fall short of addressing details on boosting income, optimism could weaken, the report said, citing Shen.

data.title: Blue Star Helium Says Flow Testing at Colorado Helium Development Well Shows Rising Natural Flow Rate
data.description:
Blue Star Helium said flow testing at the Jackson 31 SENW 3054 development well at its Galactica helium project in Colorado reached around 250 million cubic feet per day, showing increasing natural flow rates, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Strong pressure build-up after the testing indicates high permeability and good reservoir communication.

The projected stabilized flow rates constrained for production optimization are expected to be between 300 million to 400 million cubic feet per day, with a maximum of 500 million cubic feet per day.

Initial laboratory analysis of gas samples from Jackson 31 showed a helium concentration of up to 2.2%, corrected for the presence of air.

Gas samples from the well were sent for laboratory analysis to determine helium and carbon dioxide concentrations.

data.title: Uno Minda Fully Redeems INR500 Million Commercial Paper at Maturity
data.description: Auto components manufacturer Uno Minda fully redeemed commercial papers worth 500 million Indian rupees upon their maturity on Thursday, according to a same-day Indian bourse filing.

data.title: Swiss Life Says All Divisions Contributed to Strong 2024 Results
Orion Minerals Delays Release of South Africa Copper Project Definitive Feasibility Study
data.description:
Orion Minerals said the release of the definitive feasibility study for the Flat Mines copper project in South Africa was delayed to the last week of March, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The board and partners are currently working on an internal review following a sign off by the independent experts.

It is expected that the results of the Okiep definitive feasibility study will be released concurrently with the release of Orion's flagship Prieska copper-zinc mine definitive feasibility study.

data.title: French and Benelux stocks-Factors to watch
data.description:
Below are company-related news and stories from France and Benelux which could have an impact on the region's markets or individual stocks.

Believe :

Believe said in its full-year update that it was expecting adjusted EBITDA to progress in 2025 by at least 35%, and confirmed it was still guiding for positive free cash flow in 2025.

Rubis :

Rubis said in its full-year update that it sees group EBITDA between 710 and 760 million euros in 2025.

Alstom :

Alstom and SJ signed a contract for night train maintenance in Northern Sweden.

Virbac :

Virbac said it was expecting 2025 revenue growth between 4% and 6%.
